intro_script: Welcome to your daily digest for June 25, 2026! Today's scoop covers a massive new humanoid dataset, Agility Robotics going public, and Intel countering bans with next-gen development. Grab your coffee!
outro_script: And that's your robotics rundown for June 25, 2026! Keep those circuits buzzing. Thanks for joining us; may your own days be as well-trained as BitRobot’s new dataset! Bye for now!

items.title: BitRobot and Hugging Face Drop HIW-500: A Massive 10TB Real-Home Humanoid Dataset
items.summary: BitRobot, Hugging Face, and Unitree have collaborated to launch the Humanoids-in-the-Wild 500 (HIW-500) dataset. This substantial resource comprises a 500-hour teleoperation collection. The data was gathered within authentic residential environments in Southeast Asia. It was captured using Unitree G1 hardware, documenting real-world humanoid interactions. HIW-500 offers researchers vast material for training advanced humanoid AI models.
items.audio_script: From Humanoids Daily, "BitRobot and Hugging Face Drop HIW-500: A Massive 10TB Real-Home Humanoid Dataset." This report details the release of a significant new dataset designed for advancing humanoid artificial intelligence research. The resource is the Humanoids-in-the-Wild 500, or HIW-500, which represents a substantial teleoperation collection spanning five hundred hours of recorded data. This material was gathered specifically within authentic residential settings located across Southeast Asia and captured using Unitree G1 hardware, thoroughly documenting real-world humanoid operational interactions. The availability of the HIW-500 offers researchers an extensive corpus for training sophisticated humanoid AI models. Industry focus is expected to shift toward incorporating highly realistic, domestic interaction data into future robotic platform development cycles.

items.title: Humanoid maker Agility Robotics to go public through SPAC merger
items.summary: Agility Robotics plans to become publicly traded by merging with Churchill Capital Corp XI. This corporate action is set to raise $620 million in capital. Funds raised will be specifically allocated to advancing the development of the Digit v5 humanoid robot. Furthermore, the capital infusion aims to help fulfill existing and growing customer orders for its technology.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Humanoid maker Agility Robotics to go public through SPAC merger." This report details Agility Robotics' plan to achieve a public listing via a merger with Churchill Capital Corp XI. Through this corporate action, the company is slated to secure $620 million in new capital. These funds are earmarked for advancing the development cycle of the Digit v5 humanoid robot model and also intend to support existing and expanding client technology orders. The capital injection signals significant near-term investment confidence within the robotics manufacturing sector.

items.title: 1X Accelerates NEO Developer Release to Counter U.S. Bans on Chinese Humanoids
items.summary: Facing increased geopolitical pressure and the proposed GUARD Act, 1X is accelerating the release of its NEO humanoid robot. CEO Bernt Børnich announced this move to directly counter anticipated U.S. bans on Chinese robotics technology. The fast-tracking aims to address a significant hardware deficiency in the market. By speeding up developer access, 1X intends to maintain operational continuity despite potential restrictions.
items.audio_script: From Humanoids Daily, "1X Accelerates NEO Developer Release to Counter U.S. Bans on Chinese Humanoids." The report details 1X's move to fast-track the release of its NEO humanoid robot in response to heightened geopolitical tensions concerning robotics technology. CEO Bernt Børnich announced that this acceleration is a direct countermeasure against anticipated United States prohibitions on Chinese robotic systems. This rapid development aims specifically to address an identified shortage within current market hardware availability, thereby ensuring ongoing operational continuity despite potential regulatory hurdles. The industry is now focused on the immediate accessibility of NEO developer units and their impact on international deployment schedules.

items.title: Accelerating BEV Pooling on NVIDIA GPUs for Physical AI Applications
items.summary: Bird's-eye-view (BEV) projection is a prevalent design pattern in applications like autonomous vehicles, robotics, and spatial AI. These models transform raw sensor data into a top-down perspective view for better scene understanding. Efficiently processing BEV information is critical for real-time physical AI performance. Developing faster pooling mechanisms specifically designed for NVIDIA GPUs can significantly accelerate this process. This optimization boosts the speed required for demanding, real-world AI implementations.
items.audio_script: From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "Accelerating BEV Pooling on NVIDIA GPUs for Physical AI Applications." The report details advancements in optimizing Bird's-eye-view projections essential for various physical artificial intelligence deployments. Researchers focused on improving the efficiency of processing raw sensor data into a top-down scene understanding view. This involved developing and implementing faster pooling mechanisms specifically architected for execution on NVIDIA graphics processing units. These optimizations significantly increase the computational throughput required for real-time AI performance in demanding environments. The progress promises substantial boosts to the speed of complex, real-world physical AI implementations across industries.

items.title: Mantis Robotics launches dual-arm, fenceless robot
items.summary: Mantis Robotics has introduced a new dual-arm, fenceless robot system. This innovative design offers increased flexibility for configuring automation processes. Its capability allows integration into complex, real-world industrial environments. Initial coverage of the launch was provided by The Robot Report. The robot represents an advancement in adaptable automation technology.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Mantis Robotics launches dual-arm, fenceless robot." This story reports on the introduction of a novel robotic system designed to enhance flexibility in industrial automation settings. The announcement detailed a new dual-arm, fenceless robot that represents a significant leap toward adaptable automated processes. The design specifically addresses limitations inherent in traditional, confined machinery setups by offering enhanced spatial freedom. Integration into complex, real-world factory environments is indicated as a primary capability of the new hardware. This technology establishes a new benchmark for portable and configurable industrial robotics solutions across multiple sectors.

items.title: I was wary of driverless cars and their tech overlords – but they could give me a different future | Gabriel Stewart
items.summary: Self-driving cars are becoming a reality, with Waymo already testing vehicles on London's streets despite some publicized mishaps. These automated vehicles (AVs) have historically required human backup drivers but will soon operate without them. Both Waymo and British competitor Wayve plan to launch driverless minicabs in London this year, pending necessary governmental approvals. For individuals with disabilities who cannot drive, these autonomous options present significant potential benefits. Operation in a complex environment like London presents a major challenge for the emerging technology.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "I was wary of driverless cars and their tech overlords – but they could give me a different future | Gabriel Stewart." This report details the imminent integration of autonomous vehicles into complex urban settings like London. Waymo is currently testing these automated systems on city streets, marking a progression from models requiring human backup drivers to fully unsupervised operation. Both Waymo and rival British firm Wayve are scheduled to introduce driverless minicabs in London this year, contingent upon securing requisite governmental permissions. For people with disabilities unable to drive, this advancement represents notable potential utility. Operating within an environment as complicated as London poses a considerable technical challenge for these developing technologies. The industry anticipates further deployment of self-driving technology across major metropolitan areas shortly.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Rclnodejs 2.1: Native ESM for a Web-Native ROS 2 SDK
items.summary: Version 2.1.0 of rclnodejs updates the package to be fully native ESM, facilitating its use as a web-native SDK for ROS 2. This allows developers to bring ROS 2 functionality directly to the browser and modern JavaScript toolchains. The updated structure offers typed APIs via `rclnodejs/web` for direct browser connection over WebSocket. Importantly, existing CommonJS projects remain backward compatible with zero code changes. This release represents a key step toward establishing rclnodejs as a first-class web SDK while maintaining access to the full ROS 2 runtime when necessary.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Rclnodejs 2.1: Native ESM for a Web-Native ROS 2 SDK." The report details an update to the rclnodejs package that transitions it to fully native ES Modules, establishing its capacity as a web-native Software Development Kit for ROS 2. This restructuring permits direct implementation of ROS 2 functionality within browser environments and modern JavaScript toolchains by providing typed APIs through rclnodejs/web for WebSocket connections. Crucially, the release maintains backward compatibility with existing CommonJS projects without requiring any modifications to current codebases. This development signals a significant maturation point for web integration tools in robotics, enabling broader access to ROS 2 workflows directly within client-side applications.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: KRS Unleashed: Easier Hardware Acceleration Prototyping on FPGA
items.summary: Introduces KRS Unleashed, an extension of the Kria Robotic Stack framework designed for easier hardware acceleration prototyping on FPGAs with ROS 2. The tool refactors the previous complex workflow into three distinct workspaces: 'os' for firmware and sysroot creation, 'vitis' to automate vendor-driven accelerator bitstream flows via Python CLI, and 'krs' for lightweight integration. This modularization significantly improves usability and build times, potentially speeding up iteration by up to 80x compared to older methods. Users can utilize these components in isolation or together for streamlined hardware-aware ROS 2 development.
items.audio_script:
From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: KRS Unleashed: Easier Hardware Acceleration Prototyping on FPGA." The announcement details a new tool that simplifies hardware acceleration prototyping using FPGAs within the framework of ROS 2. This involves refactoring a previously complex workflow into three distinct, modular workspaces. These workspaces are designated 'os' for firmware and sysroot creation, 'vitis' to automate vendor-driven accelerator bitstream flows via Python CLI, and finally, 'krs' for lightweight integration.

This architectural reorganization significantly improves both the usability of the process and overall build times, with reports indicating potential iteration speed increases up to eighty percent compared to older methodologies. Developers can now leverage these components independently or concurrently for a more streamlined, hardware-aware ROS 2 development lifecycle. This advancement is expected to accelerate time-to-market for specialized edge computing systems utilizing field-programmable gate arrays.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Anomaly Detection. Beta testers wanted
items.summary: A system was developed to automate anomaly detection in ROS robotics by learning normal operational patterns without requiring manual threshold tuning or hardcoded rules. This system monitors a robot's topics and flags real-time deviations when something malfunctions. It has been successfully tested using public datasets covering faults on various hardware, including collaborative arms and quadrotors. The developer is now seeking beta testers to gain crucial experience monitoring the system on diverse, real-world robots. In exchange for free access and influence over development direction, the creator requests honest, detailed feedback from users in production settings.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Anomaly Detection. Beta testers wanted." A new system designed for automated anomaly detection in robotics has been developed, capable of identifying malfunctions by learning normal operational patterns without predefined thresholds or hardcoded rules. This technology monitors a robot's data topics and signals real-time deviations when performance deviates unexpectedly. Successful testing utilized public datasets covering faults across diverse hardware platforms, including collaborative arms and quadrotors. The developer is now soliciting beta testers to validate the system's performance on various operational robots in field environments. Such deployments will provide crucial experiential data for refining the detection algorithms. This advancement represents a significant step toward more robust, self-monitoring robotic systems in industrial applications.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: Embeddable React telemetry components — a browser ground station for MAVROS
items.summary: A set of MIT-licensed React components named Altara provides real-time telemetry for robotics applications, including PFDs, moving maps, gauges, and event logs. These components are designed to be embeddable and themeable, allowing for a browser-based dashboard experience within existing applications. The author demonstrated building a small drone ground station utilizing these components by connecting them to MAVROS via rosbridge. This approach aims to replace the need for separate, native Ground Control Station software. The discussion invites others who have built ROS2 browser dashboards to share their topic subscription details and challenges with standalone tool limitations.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: Embeddable React telemetry components — a browser ground station for MAVROS." The discussion introduces Altara, a set of MIT-licensed React components designed to offer real-time telemetry visualization for robotics applications directly within a web browser environment. These components facilitate the display of elements such as position and heading indicators, moving maps, gauge readouts, and event logs through embeddable and themeable modules. An author demonstrated constructing a small drone ground station by integrating these components with MAVROS via rosbridge, suggesting a path to reduce reliance on dedicated native Ground Control Station software. This development signals an increasing trend toward web-based interfaces for complex robotic control systems.

items.title: ROS Discourse General: VL53L0X Time-of-Flight sensor as a Nav2 obstacle layer (ESP32 + ROS 2 Humble)
items.summary: Details an open-source project integrating the low-cost VL53L0X Time-of-Flight sensor into the Nav2 costmap as an obstacle source for small robots. The system pipeline connects the sensor to an ESP32, streams data via serial to ROS 2, converts it to a `LaserScan` message, and feeds it into the `ObstacleLayer`. Included components are custom firmware, a serial bridge node, and a converter that synthesizes a fan from the single beam. The implementation was tested on ROS 2 Humble and provides configuration advice for setup pitfalls.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Discourse General: VL53L0X Time-of-Flight sensor as a Nav2 obstacle layer (ESP32 + ROS 2 Humble)". The article details an open-source project successfully integrating the low-cost VL53L0X Time-of-Flight sensor into the Nav2 costmap to function as an obstacle source for small robotic platforms. This pipeline involves connecting the sensor to an ESP32, streaming the resulting data serially to ROS 2, and subsequently converting that feed into a standard LaserScan message format which feeds directly into the ObstacleLayer component of the navigation stack. The methodology incorporated custom firmware development alongside a serial bridge node and a converter designed to synthesize a radial fan representation from the sensor's single beam measurement. This implementation was validated on ROS 2 Humble, offering specific guidance regarding common setup pitfalls for practitioners. Such detailed hardware integration confirms the increasing accessibility of advanced perception capabilities for resource-constrained robotics research and deployment.

items.title: LiTime Powers Smart Agriculture and Robotics in Agriculture with Extended Runtime and System Integration
items.summary: Automation is transforming smart agriculture through increased use of tools like robotic systems, automated transporters, and advanced irrigation controls on farms and greenhouses. This equipment demands extended operational runtime, intelligent management, and robust performance in harsh outdoor settings. LiTime, a leading lithium battery manufacturer, addresses these industry needs with its specialized products. The company's offerings are designed to power the growing demand for reliable, long-lasting energy solutions in agricultural automation.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "LiTime Powers Smart Agriculture and Robotics in Agriculture with Extended Runtime and System Integration." This report details how specialized battery technology is meeting the increasing power demands of automated agricultural systems. Automation is currently driving significant changes within farming, incorporating robotic units, autonomous transport mechanisms, and sophisticated irrigation controls across both open fields and controlled greenhouse environments. These advanced tools require extended operational endurance and must perform reliably even when exposed to harsh outdoor conditions, a challenge that LiTime addresses with its specialized lithium battery product lines designed for dependable, long-lasting energy provision in agricultural automation settings. The availability of reliable power sources is expected to accelerate the integration of complex robotics into global food production infrastructure.

items.title: Invariant Kalman filtering for extended pose estimation in multi-IMU articulated rigid-body systems
items.summary: Accurate extended pose estimation for multi-IMU articulated systems is challenging due to inter-body coupling and incorporating joint constraints. The authors introduce the relative L-extended pose, a Lie group representation allowing joint constraints to be expressed in an invariant form. This framework is utilized within an iterated Invariant Extended Kalman Filter (IterIEKF). IterIEKF incorporates these invariant constraints as noise-free pseudo-measurements while maintaining filtering consistency guarantees. Testing on a UR5e robot and human leg demonstrates that IterIEKF outperforms existing EKF and IterEKF baselines, achieving significantly lower RMSE and faster convergence.

items.title: ADM-Fusion: Adaptive Deep Multi-Sensor Fusion for Robust Ego-Motion Estimation in Diverse Conditions
items.summary: Developing reliable autonomy in changing environments requires adaptive multi-sensor fusion for ego-motion estimation. The proposed ADM-Fusion is an end-to-end deep learning method designed to handle environmental changes and sensor failures. It uses an adaptive sensor mixture-of-experts framework with content-aware routing to dynamically weigh different sensor inputs. Furthermore, the system includes separate translation and rotation branches connected by a cross-task attention mechanism for specialized information sharing. Testing on CARLA-LOC and KITTI data confirms ADM-Fusion's robust performance despite degraded operating conditions.

items.title: SurveilNav: Collaborative Object Goal Navigation with Robot and Surveillance System
items.summary: Collaborative object goal navigation integrating robots with surveillance systems is crucial due to expanding deployment in indoor settings. Existing methods often fail when managing multi-view collaboration across large areas. Authors introduce SurveilNav, a framework designed to utilize multi-view surveillance information for systematic evaluation. The system combines active camera scheduling, joint 2D/3D mapping, and VLM-based value estimation. Experimental results on the HM3D dataset confirm that SurveilNav achieves state-of-the-art performance in efficiency and success rates by merging dynamic robot perception with global surveillance views.

items.title: AeroCast: Probabilistic 3D Trajectory Prediction for Non-Cooperative Aerial Obstacles via Transformer-MDN Architecture
items.summary: Autonomous aerial vehicles require probabilistic methods to predict the unpredictable movements of non-cooperative obstacles like birds or debris. AeroCast introduces a framework combining a Transformer encoder with a Mixture Density Network (MDN) output head for this purpose. It predicts per-timestep Gaussian mixture distributions representing future 3D displacements, moving beyond single-point estimates. The model addresses input design and mode-degeneracy using consecutive displacement encoding and a calibration-oriented loss function. Tested on quadrotor data, AeroCast substantially reduces prediction errors while maintaining real-time inference speeds compatible with high-frequency onboard deployment.

items.title: RGB: RL Guided Whole-Body MPPI for Humanoid Control
items.summary: Develops an RL guided Whole-Body Model Predictive Path Integral (MPPI) framework for humanoid control. The method utilizes a pre-trained Reinforcement Learning (RL) policy as a sampling prior to bias MPPI rollouts towards feasible behaviors, rather than using it as the final controller. Task objectives are integrated via modular MPPI cost terms, allowing online correction of the RL prior without retraining. Simulations on a 29-DoF humanoid show stable, high-rate control (averaging 280~Hz) in MuJoCo. This approach improves task precision by correcting systematic drift and tracking additional whole-body reference signals over pure RL methods.

items.title: Causality-Based Parametric Control Barrier Function for Safe Multi-Vehicle Interaction
items.summary: Addresses challenges in safe control for autonomous driving involving multiple vehicles and varying behaviors. Existing methods struggle to model inter-vehicle influence causality or suffer from overly conservative worst-case analysis. This research extends Parametric-CBF by incorporating embedded causality inference to explicitly reason about interactions between surrounding vehicles. A resulting adaptive safety controller uses this causality-based function to allow the ego vehicle safe reactions based on learned neighbor expectations. Testing shows that utilizing motion flexibility significantly improves task efficiency in complex multi-vehicle scenarios.

items.title: Memory Retrieval in Visuomotor Policies for Long-Horizon Robot Control
items.summary: Autonomous robots in partially observable environments need general memory retrieval mechanisms to recall diverse past information for long-term control. Incorporating transformer-based memory into imitation learning faces challenges, including learning spurious correlations and compounding memory errors leading to model drift. To address this, the proposed method, HALO, integrates an attention-based memory system for visuomotor policies. It suppresses false correlations by distilling vision-language model priors using video question-answering objectives on demonstration data. Furthermore, HALO mitigates accumulated error by employing sparse attention, allowing reliable retrieval from up to eight minutes of past experience.

items.title: SwarmFly: A simulation platform for UAV swarm experiment design and validation
items.summary: SwarmFly is a new MATLAB-based simulation platform designed for designing and validating experiments involving Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarms. It addresses limitations in existing tools by offering a comprehensive, modular structure. The platform incorporates features like real-time operational maps, simulated IMU telemetry, and IP-based geolocation. SwarmFly supports four coordination modes and utilizes a plugin architecture for easy customization of behaviors and fault models. Testing confirms its capability to subject swarms to various disruptions while measuring metrics such as formation accuracy and fault recovery.

items.title: Toward Low-Latency Vision-Language Models with Doubly-Correct Predictions in Egocentric Visual Understanding
items.summary: Low-latency inference in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) is crucial for real-time human-robot collaborative tasks. Weight pruning techniques can improve VLM efficiency for on-board processing while ensuring safe human-robot interaction. Safe interaction requires preserving "doubly-correct predictions"—outputs that are both accurate and evidentially grounded. The paper introduces a rationale-informed pruning strategy because existing methods often lose prediction accuracy despite retaining evidence localization. Benchmarks confirm this new method achieves superior prediction accuracy and doubly-correct performance on egocentric video datasets.
